vectors.current_prevention: OPENCLAW_SANDBOX=1 env var in sandbox plist + tools.fs.workspaceOnly=true on infra agent. Sandbox config OPENCLAW_CONFIG points to $HOME/.openclaw/nexus-sandbox/openclaw.json — NOT prod config. Direct write from sandbox agent to prod config path is blocked by workspaceOnly guard.
vectors.fix_recommendation: None needed — workspaceOnly guard prevents direct write. Keep.

vectors.target: prod gateway config/token via openclaw doctor --fix
vectors.current_prevention: None. openclaw doctor --fix reads config pointed by OPENCLAW_CONFIG (sandbox openclaw.json) — it does NOT read prod config by default. BUT the sandbox plist sets HOME=/Users/ainchorsangiefpl, so doctor could discover $HOME/.openclaw/openclaw.json if it falls back to default config discovery. The 2026-06-08 incident showed doctor triggered token regeneration as side-effect.
vectors.fix_recommendation: 1) Add 'unset OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN' in sandbox.env so sandbox doctor cannot regenerate prod token. 2) Add OPENCLAW_SANDBOX=1 guard in any script that calls openclaw doctor.

vectors.current_prevention: Sandbox plist sets OPENCLAW_CONFIG to nexus-sandbox/openclaw.json. However sandbox.env does NOT explicitly unset OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN. If inherited from shell/launchd environment, sandbox doctor could regenerate the prod token.
vectors.fix_recommendation: Add 'unset OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN' to sandbox.env. This prevents the exact side-effect chain from INC-20260608-001.

vectors.current_prevention: Separate directories ($HOME/.openclaw/workspace-infra vs $HOME/.openclaw/workspace). Same filesystem, no mount isolation. No cross-boundary symlinks found. Risk is low because workspaceOnly guard limits file writes to workspace-infra only.
vectors.fix_recommendation: None needed currently. WorkspaceOnly guard is sufficient for filesystem isolation.

vectors.current_prevention: Sandbox plist properly isolated: OPENCLAW_CONFIG → nexus-sandbox/openclaw.json, OPENCLAW_SANDBOX=1, WorkingDirectory → nexus-sandbox, StandardOutPath/StandardErrorPath → sandbox-specific logs. Port 28789 (sandbox convention per CHG-0471).
vectors.fix_recommendation: Minor hardening: add 'unset OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN' to EnvironmentVariables dict in plist (belt-and-suspenders).

vectors.current_prevention: Cron payloads are managed by production gateway (port 18789). Sandbox gateway (port 28789) runs its own infra agent. No sandbox agentTurn crons exist. Subagent spawns inherit parent workspace restriction.
vectors.fix_recommendation: None needed. Audit confirmed no crons run through sandbox context.

vectors.current_prevention: No OPENCLAW_DB, PGHOST, PGPASSWORD, or postgres connection params found in nexus-sandbox/ env. The sandbox has no DB access path. Prod auto-heal writes to state_autoheal_log via db-raw.sh on port 18789 — sandbox has no equivalent.
vectors.fix_recommendation: None needed. If sandbox ever needs DB access, use a read-only mirror or dedicated sandbox schema per TKT-0396.

vectors.source: Any openclaw doctor --fix invocation from sandbox context
vectors.target: Production gateway SIGTERM via token mismatch
vectors.current_prevention: Sandbox plist points OPENCLAW_CONFIG to nexus-sandbox/openclaw.json. BUT the doctor command discovers gateway token via OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN env var. If this env var leaks from user's shell (common on macOS via launchctl setenv), sandbox process could regenerate the prod token.
vectors.fix_recommendation: Same fix as A1.2/A1.3 — explicitly unset OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN in sandbox.env.
